headline: Peas 9 days out, trellis 3 days late
weather_notes: Stake blackberry and peach before Tuesday — wind gusts to 33 mph could uproot or snap 2-day-old transplants.
Provide afternoon shade for peach today — logged as struggling at planting and 89°F today is peak heat stress before roots can establish.
Pick c2–c4 peas before Tuesday's heat — 89°F today and 86°F tomorrow accelerate pod toughening with harvest just 9 days out.
Hold manual watering through Wednesday — Rachio already ran zones 1 and 2 this morning and Wednesday's 20% storm chance may cover any deficit.

surfaced_tasks.title: Install trellis in c1 bean bed
surfaced_tasks.reason: Three days overdue — Good Mother Stollard pole beans are 22 days old and today's 89°F heat surges vine growth daily without support.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due June 1 (7 days) and strawberries at 30 days post-plant are entering peak fruiting season when nitrogen matters most.

suggested_tasks.title: Pick ripe pods from c2–c4 peas
suggested_tasks.description: Pods formed heavily by May 12 and harvest is 9 days out — 89°F today will toughen unpicked pods fast.

suggested_tasks.title: Rig shade cloth over peach mid-afternoon
suggested_tasks.description: Logged as 'looking really rough' at planting 2 days ago — 89°F today risks sunscald and transplant failure before roots can establish.
